data.narrative:
Dream Security, a Series B defense and security company founded by Shalev Hulio and Sebastian Kurz, is currently valued at $1.5B post-money following $100M+ in total fundraising. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $1.5B last post-money valuation, implying $2.9B. Peer-median analysis across 5 same-sector, same-stage comparables—Vannevar Labs ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Castelion ($1.0B), Defense Unicorns ($1.0B), and Heven AeroTech ($1.0B)—yields a median of $1.0B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B.

These methods support a triangulated valuation range of $850M (low) to $3.3B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around the company's positioning relative to peers and the appropriate stage-progression multiple. The peer-median implies significant near-term upside embedded in the $1.5B entry valuation, while stage-progression suggests higher medium-term expectations. Resolution of this tension depends on Dream Security's revenue trajectory, customer diversification, and retention relative to the peer set over the next funding cycle.
